policy_name: NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 - Protecting Controlled Unclassified Information
purpose: Apply NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 requirements to classify permissions that interact with Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) or CUI-adjacent resources. Focuses on the Access Control (3.1.x) and System and Communications Protection (3.13.x) requirement families. Resources tagged with CUI, PII, PHI, or SENSITIVE are subject to heightened classification.

classification_model.privileged: Any permission that grants create, modify, delete, or access-control management over a CUI-tagged resource is Privileged under 3.1.1 and 3.1.2.
classification_model.risky: Any permission that allows bulk read, export, or copy of CUI-tagged resources, or any permission lacking encryption-in-transit requirements, is Risky under 3.13.8.

rules.control_ref: 3.1.1: Limit system access to authorised users, processes acting on behalf of authorised users, and devices
rules.name: CUI Resource Access by Unauthorised Principal Type
rules.description: 3.1.1 requires that access to CUI resources be limited to explicitly authorised users. A wildcard principal ('*', 'allUsers', 'allAuthenticatedUsers') granted access to any resource tagged as CUI directly violates this requirement.
rules.rationale: A wildcard principal grants access to any identity that presents valid credentials, including external parties and unauthenticated users. CUI must only be accessible to named, authorised principals.
rules.remediation: Remove wildcard principals from all policies governing CUI-tagged resources. Replace with explicit named principals or groups with documented authorisation.

rules.control_ref: 3.1.2: Limit system access to types of transactions and functions authorised users are permitted to execute
rules.name: CUI Resource with Write or Delete Access
rules.description: 3.1.2 requires that authorised users are limited to transaction types appropriate to their role. Write and delete access to CUI-tagged resources must be restricted to principals with a documented need to modify CUI — read-only access is the default authorisation for most principals.
rules.rationale: Write access to CUI-tagged resources can result in data corruption, injection of false records, or cover-up of unauthorised access via overwrites.
rules.remediation: Decompose CUI access into read-only and write-capable roles. Assign write/delete roles only to principals with a documented need and management approval.

rules.control_ref: 3.1.3: Control the flow of CUI in accordance with approved authorisations
rules.name: CUI Data Exfiltration Risk - Bulk Read or Export
rules.description: 3.1.3 requires that CUI flow be controlled. Permissions that allow bulk read (List + GetObject on '*', Scan on a database, SelectObjectContent) on CUI-tagged resources create an exfiltration risk — a single permission can expose the entire CUI dataset.
rules.rationale: Bulk read on CUI resources enables the extraction of an entire CUI dataset in a single API call. Even legitimate principals with bulk-read rights pose an exfiltration risk if their credentials are compromised.
rules.remediation: Restrict bulk-read actions to named prefixes or specific object keys. Enable VPC endpoint policies to prevent exfiltration to non-authorised destinations. Monitor for anomalous read volume.

rules.control_ref: 3.1.5: Employ the principle of least privilege, including for specific security functions and privileged accounts
rules.name: Privileged Access to CUI Resource Without Explicit Authorisation
rules.description: 3.1.5 requires least privilege to be applied specifically to security functions and privileged accounts. Any admin, manage-permissions, or delete action on a CUI-tagged resource is a privileged access event and must be explicitly authorised.
rules.rationale: Privileged access to CUI resources requires explicit authorisation beyond normal access controls. The combination of privileged action + CUI data is the highest-risk classification.
rules.remediation: Require management approval, MFA, and session recording for all privileged access to CUI resources. Log every action with the approver's identity.

rules.control_ref: 3.13.8: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorised disclosure of CUI during transmission
rules.name: CUI Resource Accessible Over Unencrypted Transport
rules.description: 3.13.8 requires cryptographic protection of CUI in transit. A permission that grants access to a CUI resource without requiring encrypted transport (TLS/HTTPS) in the policy condition allows the data to be accessed over unencrypted channels.
rules.rationale: Unencrypted access to CUI in transit enables passive interception (man-in-the-middle). Even internal network traffic must be encrypted for CUI under 800-171.
rules.remediation: Add a Deny policy statement with Condition: {Bool: {aws:SecureTransport: false}} on all CUI-tagged S3 buckets and endpoints. Enforce TLS 1.2 minimum.

rules.control_ref: 3.13.16: Protect the confidentiality of CUI at rest
rules.name: CUI Resource Without Encryption-at-Rest Condition
rules.description: 3.13.16 requires protection of CUI at rest. A write permission to a CUI-tagged resource without requiring server-side encryption (SSE) headers allows unencrypted data to be stored.
rules.rationale: Without a Deny condition on unencrypted puts, callers can write plaintext objects to CUI-tagged buckets. Physical or logical storage access then exposes the data.
rules.remediation: Add a Deny statement: {Effect: Deny, Action: s3:PutObject, Condition: {Null: {s3:x-amz-server-side-encryption: true}}} on all CUI buckets. Enforce SSE-KMS with a customer-managed key.
